meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use email automation to overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first project.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop building tutorials and start shipping utilities. By automating email workflows, you demonstrate real-world data processing skills. Use Antigravity to scaffold your backend and Claude Code to iterate on logic, transforming a simple script into a professional-grade SaaS tool for your resume.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank GitHub profile, haunted by the ghost of 'To-Do List' tutorials that impress no one. The industry doesn't care about your theoretical knowledge; they care about your ability to solve messy, real-world problems. The gap between a student and a developer isn't intelligence—it's the evidence of shipping functional code.

Email automation is the ultimate 'low-code' bridge to high-impact engineering. By building a system that triggers intelligent responses or CRM updates, you aren't just writing scripts; you're building business infrastructure. This guide is your blueprint to turning a weekend project into a portfolio piece that commands attention.

steps.title: Architecting the Workflow
steps.description: Define the trigger and the action. We will build a service that listens for incoming emails and uses an LLM to categorize the intent before routing it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Node.js backend using Express that integrates with an IMAP library to listen for incoming emails and structure the payload for an LLM processing pipeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project folder, initialize package.json, and install node-imap and dotenv'

steps.title: Engineering the Intelligence Layer
steps.description: Integrate Claude to parse email sentiment and extract key entities, turning raw text into actionable JSON data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function that sends email body text to the Anthropic API, requesting a JSON response containing sentiment, urgency, and categorized intent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a service/llm.js file that exports a function to classify email content using the Anthropic SDK'

steps.title: Implementing the Automation Logic
steps.description: Connect the processed data to a destination, such as a Google Sheet or a Slack channel, to demonstrate real-world utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a handler that takes the LLM-processed JSON and updates a Google Sheet via the Google Sheets API with the email metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Install google-spreadsheet and implement an appendRow function to log categorized emails'

steps.title: Robust Error Handling
steps.description: Professional code doesn't just work; it fails gracefully. We'll add logging and retry logic for network timeouts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a robust error-handling decorator to the email processing pipeline that logs failures to a local file and retries connection on network jitter.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review 'Check the current flow for error handling and suggest a retry strategy with exponential backoff'

steps.title: Deployment & Documentation
steps.description: Package your project for production and write a README that highlights the business value of your automation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that explains the architecture, the problem solved, and instructions for local environment setup.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m 'Finalize automation logic and add comprehensive README documentation'

pro_tips: Always log the raw input before processing; it makes debugging production issues 10x faster.
Use environment variables for everything; never hardcode credentials, even in your first project.
Focus on the 'Why' in your README; explain the business problem you solved, not just the tech stack.
Implement 'Dry Run' mode to test your automation without actually sending emails or writing to production databases.
Include a 'Future Improvements' section in your README to show that you think like a product owner, not just a coder.
content.next_steps: After completing this, deploy your tool to a free-tier cloud host like Render or Railway. Once live, post a 30-second demo video on LinkedIn tagging the tools you used to build it.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-ready automation tool that solves a real business problem—the exact type of project that separates junior developers from the rest of the pack. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
